key_points: The production of a shirt involves several stages including cotton farming, spinning, weaving, and stitching
Many people such as farmers, traders, factory workers, and retailers participate in the production chain
Workers in garment factories often receive low wages compared to the final selling price of shirts
Large companies and brands control marketing and distribution in the market
Global markets influence how clothes are produced and sold
examples: Cotton farmers grow and sell raw cotton to traders
Spinning mills convert cotton into yarn
Weaving units produce cloth from yarn
Garment factories stitch shirts that are later branded and sold in markets
International brands export shirts to many countries
formulas: Production Chain → Sequence of processes involved in making a product
Raw Material → Basic material used to produce goods
Value Addition → Increase in product value at each stage of production
exam_tips: Understand each stage of shirt production from cotton to finished product
Remember the role of different workers in the production chain
Focus on how profits are distributed among producers and companies
Use examples to explain economic inequality in production
